title: [FIRRTL] Canonicalization breaks preservation of named wires
body:
```scala
circuit Foo :
  module Foo :
    input a : UInt<1>
    output b : UInt<1>

    wire x: UInt<1>
    x <= a 
    node y = x 
    b <= y 
```

Current output:
```verilog
  assign x = a; 
  assign y = a;
  assign b = y;  
```

Expecte result: 
```verilog
  assign x = a;
  assign y = x;
  assign b = y; 
```

Interestingly, if we change x into node, we can get the expected outcome. 
```scala
circuit Foo :
  module Foo :
    input a : UInt<1>
    output b : UInt<1>

    node x = a 
    node y = x 
    b <= y 
```
